definition: Canonical three-marks formula stating the not-self character of all phenomena.
notes: Use this record for the full formula line. Dhp 279 anchors the line itself. It is a controlled override of the headword-level preference to leave dhamma untranslated: here the broad analytic scope is better heard as phenomena. The Buddhadasa usage profile supports keeping anattā practical and anti-reifying, so the line should stay within the repository's governed not-self family rather than being inflated into metaphysical no-self rhetoric.

context_rules.context: When the surrounding passage is deliberately source-facing
context_rules.notes: Controlled alternate for passages that intentionally retain dhamma.

example_phrases.notes: Controlled formula-level override of the headword default for dhamma.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the canonical formula line itself.
Supports keeping anattā practical and anti-reifying rather than turning the formula into nihilistic no-self rhetoric.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use as the standard rendering for the full Dhp 279 three-marks line.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not let the formula's use of phenomena force every dhamma context into the same English, and do not recast anattā as no-self.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents collapse of the formula into either untranslated dhamma everywhere or nihilistic no-self slogans.
